Package com.bonitasoft.engine.api

Interface DelegationAPI

public interface DelegationAPI
Public Java API for managing task delegation in Bonita Subscription edition.

A DelegationRule grants a delegate user visibility and execution rights on the delegator's human tasks during a bounded delegation period, without reassigning ownership. A user may hold at most one rule at a time; calling createOrUpdateDelegation(DelegationRuleCreator) replaces any existing rule for the delegator (upsert semantics).

Authorization

Administrators may target any delegator; non-administrators may only manage their own delegation. When DelegationRuleCreator.setDelegatorId(long) is not called, the delegator defaults to the authenticated session user.

Note: Authorization enforcement is provided by the permission layer in a follow-up PR. Until that layer lands, callers must not rely on the API itself to reject unauthorized requests.

Search

Search methods accept standard SearchOptions with filter keys defined in DelegationRuleSearchDescriptor and DelegatedTaskSearchDescriptor. Results are returned as public business objects (DelegationRule, DelegatedTask); engine-internal entities are never exposed.
Since:
11.1
See Also:
  • DelegationRule
  • DelegatedTask
  • DelegationRuleCreator
  • DelegationRuleUpdater
  • DelegationRuleSearchDescriptor
  • DelegatedTaskSearchDescriptor

  • Method Details

    • createOrUpdateDelegation

      DelegationRule createOrUpdateDelegation(DelegationRuleCreator creator) throws CreationException
      Creates or replaces the delegation rule for a delegator (upsert).

      If the delegator already has a rule, it is replaced atomically. Otherwise a new rule is created. The four mandatory fields (delegateId, startDate, endDate, processes) are provided by the DelegationRuleCreator constructor; the optional delegatorId defaults to the session user.

      Parameters:
      creator - the creator carrying the rule fields; must be non-null and contain at least one process in its whitelist
      Returns:
      the created or updated rule, with server-assigned fields populated (id, lastUpdatedBy, lastUpdatedAt, derived status)
      Throws:
      CreationException - if the rule cannot be persisted (e.g. invalid date range, empty process whitelist, delegator and delegate are the same user, unknown delegate or delegator, or any other engine error)
      InvalidSessionException - if the API session is invalid or expired

    • updateDelegation

      DelegationRule updateDelegation(long ruleId, DelegationRuleUpdater updater) throws UpdateException, NotFoundException
      Partially updates an existing delegation rule.

      Only the fields explicitly set on the DelegationRuleUpdater are modified; unset fields are preserved. The updater must carry at least one field — an empty updater is rejected with UpdateException; there is no implicit no-op update.

      Parameters:
      ruleId - the identifier of the rule to update
      updater - the updater carrying the fields to modify; must be non-null and carry at least one field
      Returns:
      the updated rule
      Throws:
      NotFoundException - if no rule with the given id exists
      UpdateException - if the updater is empty, the start/end date range is invalid, the new process whitelist is empty, the delegate is unknown, or any other engine error occurs
      InvalidSessionException - if the API session is invalid or expired

    • deleteDelegation

      void deleteDelegation(long ruleId) throws DeletionException
      Deletes a delegation rule.

      Idempotent: deleting an already-deleted rule completes silently without throwing NotFoundException, matching the behavior of DELETE REST semantics.

      Parameters:
      ruleId - the identifier of the rule to delete
      Throws:
      DeletionException - if the rule exists but cannot be deleted (engine error)
      InvalidSessionException - if the API session is invalid or expired

    • searchDelegationRules

      SearchResult<DelegationRule> searchDelegationRules(SearchOptions options) throws SearchException
      Searches delegation rules with pagination, sorting and filtering.

      Filter keys come from DelegationRuleSearchDescriptor. The DelegationRuleSearchDescriptor.STATUS key is a virtual filter computed at query time from startDate / endDate against the current timestamp and accepts DelegationStatus values.

      Parameters:
      options - the search options (pagination, sort, filters); must be non-null
      Returns:
      a paginated result of DelegationRule matching the criteria
      Throws:
      SearchException - if the search cannot be executed (e.g. invalid filter key, engine error)
      InvalidSessionException - if the API session is invalid or expired

    • searchDelegatedTasks

      SearchResult<DelegatedTask> searchDelegatedTasks(SearchOptions options) throws SearchException
      Searches human tasks that the current user is allowed to act on through an active DelegationRule, with pagination, sorting and filtering.

      Filter keys come from DelegatedTaskSearchDescriptor. Only tasks belonging to processes that are part of an active rule's whitelist are returned.

      The returned DelegatedTask instances expose denormalized delegator / delegate User objects; if a referenced user no longer exists (deactivated), a synthetic placeholder with only the id set is returned and a warning is logged.

      Parameters:
      options - the search options (pagination, sort, filters); must be non-null
      Returns:
      a paginated result of DelegatedTask matching the criteria
      Throws:
      SearchException - if the search cannot be executed (e.g. invalid filter key, engine error)
      InvalidSessionException - if the API session is invalid or expired
